reviews.review_text:
I liked the second game in this trilogy a lot and thus I was eager to play Shadow of the Tomb Raider. Yet, unfortunetly, I did not reach the same highs as the previous game.

The atmosphere is stunning, yet it got disturbed by an enormous amount of bugs. Many bugs were visual and audial, which broke the immersion a lot, and there was even one bug that hindered me passing a puzzle. The latter one is simply to say unaccpetable...

Besides that, I realy enjoyed the stealth mechanic! It was simple, yet tied with the atmosphere, it really did it for me. 

Those were the high and the lowlights of this game I would say, other parts were just good or fine at best. Consequently, I would not recommend this game, primarily due to the amount of bugs in it

in this game the focus was clearly put on cinematics and direction. the characters feel more believable, more realistic, and it’s much easier to emotionally trust them.

but in exchange for all of that, the game completely lost its original charm. there’s no longer that amazing level design, interesting tombs or excitement from exploring the world. the game basically turned into a regular story-driven experience.

weapon and skill progression - meh. i never actually felt the need to upgrade much even on hard mode. there was this constant feeling that the game got heavily casualized to attract a bigger audience.

yeah, the game looks beautiful and the cinematography is great. the story is dynamic, compact and the characters are well developed. but for every thing that got better, something else got worse.

i love this series too much to leave a negative review, but i really hope the developers will understand their strengths and weaknesses better when making Legacy of Atlantis.
A fantastic storyline and tons of collectibles. I had a blast playing this game. The graphics are simply a masterpiece. In fact, the game was a pioneer in the use of ray tracing.

For the first 10 hours, I was really confused why this was considered the worst in the trilogy, sure some of the side missions are pointless filler, but once I got to Paititi, that's when the cracks started to show. For those who are unaware Crystal Dynamics was about halfway through the development of this game, until the publisher Square Enix got the license to make the marvel's avengers game. Riding high on the peak popularity of the marvel movies, they tasked Crystal Dynamics to make that game and assigned Eidos-Montreal to finish this one. And it clearly shows. In the end this move was a terrible decision as the avengers game lost the publisher $200 million dollars as the game was terrible.

The story starts off really good, picking up where Rise left off, the graphics in its lush environments are beautiful exploration is the best the series has to offer. The tombs are hands down the best in the series as well also has a lot of time and attention to detail in its puzzles. And while it can be tedious to go back and collect what you missed, there is so much variation in its environments It didn't bother me too much. Especially with difficult options for puzzles, exploration, and combat its nice to have some of these more challenging puzzles have some hints if you are struggling.

Its combat is a ton of fun with a lot of new things added, like fear arrows to make enemies commit friendly fire before dying. The mud camouflage to blend with the environment, climbing up trees and either do an aerial takedown or use rope arrows to hang them from the tree. More guns to get with various upgrades, with gold to purchase things like ammo pouches, increases, and attachments, though the shotgun is pretty underwhelming. However, as people have said, there isn't a lot of combat throughout the game compared to the first one, which is a shame. It's a waste to use your skill points for combat early in the game, as it’s better used for other skills. It's also really frustrating that enemies don’t respawn in an area you revisit to add some additional combat, and worst of all, unlike Rise, there is no level selector or combat mode, so if you want to fight some more, you have to replay the entire game over. All of these cool mechanics, like the mud stealth that took a lot of time and effort to create and playtest is barely used in comparison to what you will be doing in the game. The final boss was one of the most boring and underwhelming I have experienced in a long time. Yeah, the arenas look cool, but it boils down to run and gun dodging his attacks and shoot enough times to melee him rinse and repeat like 5 times, and that's it. 

Also, the game is way too easy on hard. I get that there is a harder mode above that, but it's only available on New Game Plus, and most people won’t do that, so it makes the game kinda easy, especially when you carry so many healing berries by the end. Because of this, the other things like using other berries to increase the slow down aim time or perception vision all feel pointless as i never actually used them, unless you want to 100% everything, the game is easy enough to navigate as it is.

Its story is the worst part of this game, as i said it starts off really good and I was hoping for the same level of quality as Rise did (despite that game having some pointless filler, it was still excellent), but once you get to Paititi it all falls apart. Initially, it was fine as Laura believed they had the box, but once she realized they didn’t have it that should have been the moment to move on. But no she stays and helps people with their mundane problems, as they exposition dump the tribe’s culture. It would have been fine, but the writing isn't very good as it gets boring and confusing as they mix people, places, and concepts, some you get to see in the game, and other things you don’t. In the context of how this game starts, I don’t care because I wasn’t made to care, as all we are after is the box. They throw a curveball that the villain is somehow closely connected with Paititi, and Trinity controls them. This felt like this connection was thrown together at the last minute in an attempt to make this part of the game make sense. The flashbacks of Laura as a kid were a nice touch. Also, they did my boy Jonah so dirty in this one; they get split up a lot and always seem to make it to where I take the long, dangerous path. It isn't until the last quarter of the game that he does anything to help Laura at all. And that ending, nothing makes sense, nothing is explained, nothing feels like it connects from the story prior, and you are left confused by the end as the credits roll. I did about 85% of the game and there was still a couple side missions left and some things to pick up but there isn't any incentive to 100% it 

Overall, it isn't a terrible game, but this is definitely the weakest out of the three. I’d say it's still worth playing for its exploration and tombs, as that is the best part of this game. The trilogy constantly goes on sale for all of them for $10 - $15 its absolutely worth getting the bundle for that price.

I 100%ed this game so here is my review: in search of looking good I believe there wasn't much work put on the story. Characters feel shallow and there're a lot of them, hard to even keep track and also a bunch of NPCs that have a special ability to annoy you. That being said, if you rush it it can become a better experience solely because you'll have more fight sequences. I played in the hardest mode, don't do that, it made me drop the game in the middle of it because I kept failing a stupid parkour segment (something that happens quite a lot). Why did I 100% it then? Honor. I just couldn't bear the fact that a game like that could make me quit so I did everything. Now, it has good moments, the DLC is nice (a bit buggy but nice) and the tombs are an upgrade from the other games of the trilogy. The bad part is mostly the story and the movement and the fact that Lara is stupid.

The best Tomb Raider series so far. Beautiful graphics that provide an adventurous experience and endless finding of materials in nature. Very detailed design. All credit to the Square Enix studio. I really enjoyed the game and indulged in the charms of archaeological research, and a little bit of Lara Croft ( ;) ). The humor aside, the game really provides a phenomenal atmosphere and detailed environment that keeps the player's attention, especially when they have the opportunity to explore tombs and sanctuaries at their own will in addition to the main mission. A warm recommendation for those who love adventure, archaeological sites, history, crafting, survival and exploration - this is the game for you.

Dreadful, never been less invested in a character. Endless cut scenes, average story, terrible puzzles, couldn't care less about the various outfits, awful close up combat (thankfully of which there's very little). 3 / 10, actually 2.
actually this game is very very good but the bug in path of the living puzzle ruinedd everything

I encountered a bug in Shadow of the Tomb Raider during the “Path of the Living” section. The game becomes stuck and progression is impossible. I tried changing graphics settings, verifying game files, and even completely uninstalling and reinstalling the game, but the issue still persists.

This bug prevents me from continuing the story and significantly affects the gameplay experience. I hope the developers can investigate and provide a fix.


Thank you.

It's a beautiful game and I recommend buying it. I didn't regret buying it at all; it deserves all the praise. It has a lovely story and puzzles that are neither too difficult not too easy; you just need to concentrate and use a little imagination to solve them. And with that, I finished the trilogy. Thank you to the creators of this game.

You know what, all three of this new Tomb Raider trilogy are fantastic! The first one was a bit clunky gameplay-wise but the second and third improved everything so much it's insane.
The DLC missions were also very good in this one - quite a lot of them as well which was a surprise.
Strongly recommended if you enjoyed either of the first two games from this series or Tomb Raider in general!

I do not recommend getting this game, even on sale.

The cutscenes often play without audio, or out of sync audio.
The graphical errors are numerous.
Locking the camera often means you can't climb down from a ledge to a climable wall, or you end up jumping way out when jumping from a climable wall and missing the other wall (and falling to your death).
Quite often doesn't grab ledges, walls, poles when you hit E.
Lara's character assassination was BRUTAL. She's so disinterested in the lives of others, and written in a way that makes her seem naive, even though she's already had 2 games prior showcasing growth in understanding, knowledge, and how to handle these missions.

The first two games in the series had their quirks, but I was able to happily play them to 100% completion. I finished the main storyline of this game and instantly uninstalled. The ONLY reason I continued to play it was because I was streaming it. I could not, in good conscience, recommend this game unless you hate yourself.

Gameplay: Really fun compared to ROTTR. Puzzles and Tombs galore which were surprisingly challenging. Big Issue: Replayed the same chapter countless times because of a glitch and even AFTER it fixed, Lara was sent to space and I had to redo the SAME CHAPTER from before and the glitch continued. Downloaded a save state off Nexus to help me get past it because ♥♥♥♥ this game. 
Story: Lara gets away with genocide and no one cares. 
Graphics: Really beautiful. Wasted potential since it focuses on one hidden village the size of South America. 
Music: Loved the soundtrack but wasn't memorable
Lara: IS SO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ANNOYING 
Jonah: Wish he had a solo game

I keep getting glitch where the chase scene with the Yaaxil. Halfway through, I kept getting cut right into a death scene where Lara gets pummelled by them. I watched other playthroughs and they took their time running around whereas I kept getting death scenes. I had already replay the chapter over and over again only to keep getting the same results. While I don't mind getting restarted back onto base camps every time Lara dies. Being restarted due to unresponsiveness of the game and glitches and have to go through insufferable and unskippable cutscenes is very frustrating, a great way to waste your time on a game and a lazy way for devs to reel your time onto their game. I'm done with this game and will be on a lookout for this developer from now on.
The issue with this game is they were so hyper focused on the graphics they let the latter half the the story fall apart.
Just finished it. It's amazing, beautiful, warm and is worth shedding a tear. I really recommend it! Loved it,

90%
+ Incredible map/world detail
+ Lara/Jonah relationship feels meaningful and drives the story
+ Best version of TR gameplay, perfect balance in stealth/gunfights
+ Fantastic visuals
+ Best puzzles of the series
+ Lots of potential for exploration/adventure
- Mixed ending, good conclusion for Lara Croft but not to SoTR story
- Crafting mechanic avoided
- Many elements of skill tree not useful

Peak story, peak dub, peak combat, peak scene,
everything is PEAK
Personally, I thought it was gonna be a slop game, but it turned out to be an unexpectedly peak game.
it is all out Tomb Rider game, where every detail is perfectly brewed, 

Score 10/10 literally
currently will replay max diff for achievement
Most stupid game play I have ever encountered. Never again. ♥♥♥♥ you lot, totally!!

This game is severely underrated. With serious pacing and story issues, it's easy for some to overlook the still gorgeous graphics, fantastic climbing and discovery aspects of the game. Especially on PC, with mods like classic Lara outfits or the stunning Eclipse mod, overhauling looks and some dialogue, this one is as close as we ever got to the real tomb raiding experience in the trilogy. Definitely give it a try, and don't miss the DLC tombs, they're some of the best this game has to offer.
Pure Tomb Raider. Much adventure and exploration to be had. The most abilities at her disposal and a good enough story, if you're into South American culture.
one of the best adventurous game i ever played and i love the jungle vibe.

As the conclusion to the latest Lara Croft trilogy, it left me wanting more. The constant presence and unsettling sounds of the Yaaxil often made it feel more like a horror game than an action-adventure. While I'm glad I experienced it, I had hoped for a more impactful resolution to Lara's quest for vengeance against her father's killer.
Worst TR game, 90% cutscene you cant skip and 10% climbing.

Shadow of the Tomb Raider is good. Not great, not bad, just good.

The graphics and environment are genuinely incredible. I'd even argue Shadow is probably the best tomb raider game when it comes to visuals. Everything feels "alive" and realistic, plus some of the views are genuinely breath taking. There's multiple tombs (that are not just press this and jump), a lot of collectibles, side missions and challenges to complete, so the map doesn't even feel empty. The tombs also gave new skills and outfits which worked as motivation to fully explore everything.

The combat is interesting but heavily favours stealth, which I didn't mind necessarily, but it made some sections annoyingly frustrating as the enemies only needed maybe two strikes to almost kill you. The stealth sections were very fun though and seeing Lara coat herself in mud to better camouflage herself in the environment was a nice touch that made her feel more powerful.

Cosmetics were disappointing. The recent tomb raider games have always had a lot of outfits and while Shadow had multiple, being able to use them freely was not possible. You are mostly restricted to the tribal outfits when in Paititi, and a lot of them do not look... the best. A new gameplay function was being able to craft a top-part and lower-leg-part and mix-n-match them, which was okayish, but I'd have liked having more customization options for them since they were just added on-top of the core outfit.

The story is decent but unfortunately really slows down during the paititi section (and some sections feel very unbelievable), but it's not bad. No spoilers, but it follows a similar premise to Rise which is a shame, since it causes the game feel more repetitive. At least the side missions were interesting and unique. One of them gave a LOT of insight into Lara's character and how she feels about herself on the inside, and I would've liked to see more of that side in the main story as well.

tldr: Visuals are stunning, combat is good but favours stealth, cosmetics are somewhat lacking and story could've been better. I'd still recommend it especially if its on sale as I enjoyed it a lot regardless of my complaints
ill keep it short and sweet.  if you play any of the tomb roader reboots and you enjoyed them this will add to  the rest.  this game does not dissappoint.

Fix the Pisco the dead glitch it's been almost a decade, all the devs have to do is make him non interactive until you talk to the boy first??? At least put some kind of warning? Add an autosave when you enter the village? ANYTHING to avoid having to completely restart a game which features an insane amount of un-skippable content. Shadow does feel a bit rushed compared to the previous games from the survivor trilogy but this specific bug is so sloppy to an otherwise enjoyable game.
its an 10/10 game, lots to explore, but if you like story games this is a great story game, there's puzzels, temples to explore, places to go, parkour to figure out, action and adventure
Pretty good game, it's quite short so maybe buy it on a sale but it has some solid segments, especially some of the stealth segments.
a few bugs here an there but overall nice story game. Make sure to play on higher difficulty so gathering and hunting for resources stays interesting.

[h1]A must-play for Tomb Raider fans![/h1]

[b]Graphics & Visuals:[/b] The graphics are much better than the previous games, and Lara has a more mature look.

[b]The Jungle Environment:[/b] About 90% of the game is set in jungles. It feels so realistic that you might actually get lost!

[b]Story & Gameplay:[/b] The story and gameplay are a bit weaker compared to the earlier titles.

[hr][/hr]
[b]Final Verdict:[/b] Despite some weak points, I highly recommend this game to all Tomb Raider and gaming enthusiasts.

well its Tomb Raider.. and a very good continuation from the previous re-imagined games. its the same but with polish, from inventory to combat just better. the story and the environment again, polished, Rise of TR was better but this, if you like TR is as good as it gets until we get "Atlantis" and the old but new Lara, fingers crossed.
I didnt like it, story-wise and gameplay-wise. I was expecting for it to be as good as uncharted but not. Not even close.
Great Game. Great Storyline and Graphics. Lost of places to explore and discover.

the mechanism in the game got  stuck now im stuck  in that chapter and cant move to the other because no matter how i try to restart the game, the mechanism just doesnt work.  i dont know  what to do. can someone help me with this?

The 2nd oil puzzle has to be one of the most frustrating puzzles to ever exist. The puzzle kept over ridding my controls and makes what should be a rather simple puzzle so angering that I ended up breaking my controller and uninstalling the game.

PSA: CAP FRAMERATE TO 120 (maybe even 60) VIA GRAPHICS CARD CONTROL PANEL. There are some sections of the game that become impossible if the framerate is too high.

Review: For $6 (current sale), sure. It feels like they put 99% of the budget, passion, time, into art design and lore and 1% into gameplay. The art design is amazing. The levels are beautiful and detailed. The exploration and gameplay is pretty underwhelming. The game has crashed a few times during the DLC as well. The game feels like a chore after a while.

Also the game is a loading screen hiding simulator. I hope you like squeezing through little crevices really slowly, walking through mud really slowly, or randomly seeing Lara set all of her gear on the ground and inspecting her pistol for no reason every time you enter a new area. But hey, ✨No Loading Screens!✨

The only reason I'm saying "yes" to recommending the game is that it frequently goes on sale with a heavy discount.

Easily the worst game in the trilogy, countless bugs and glitches, culminating in a final mission with completely muted audio and a super underwhelming platforming section culminating in a lacklustre "bossfight"
After playing the whole series !! I can say this is best female protaganist series ever

The game is just fun, Graphics are amazing, even on a lower type PC it can run max graphics on 110+ Frames. The story is Okay at best but besides that the Gameplay is really fun and engaging. especially the stealth in this game is really amazing! overall i would give the game like a 8.5/10!
you get transferred into nicely made, realistic world

I will start of with Yes, Rise is the better game.  However, I did enjoy this.  

The game did suffer in Development hell and it does show in certain ways.  The story with Trinity doesn't really go anywhere (new head writer for story) and the facial animations with Lara are a step back from Rise.  

That being said, I'm a sucker for games set in a Jungle/Forest type setting.  Maybe I just love the color green... The game is gorgeous.  The focus being more on stealth felt a little better and more realistic to how This current Lara would approach an armed conflict situation.  The Rambo style of Rise was enjoyable albeit feeling a little over the top (but still fun regardless).  There are plenty of tombs to explore with decent puzzles.  That might be why I enjoyed this a tad more than Rise due to the focus being on exploration this time.  

If you're looking for more exploration and less combat in your TR game and your not super invested in the story being fleshed out from the previous two games, than this is a good choice.

Great adventure and definetly the best one out of the three

Lara gets way more character development and it is actually interesting to follow.
Graphics are great and its hella optimized, never had a frame drop in 10 hours.
Jungles and set pieces are colorful and enjoyable to look at.
Changes of pace are good too and you usually don't get bored.

While being somewhat repetative story-wise, it is actually a decent improvement in every aspect and i'm definetly recommending this one. Short. Fun. Intersting.

I have played this game on console, on a potato pc, a broken thinkpad, on a gaming laptop with rtx 1650, now on my legitimate Gaming PC. This game never stops to impress it is so well optimized that it ran perfectly fine on almost all the devices I played on on low setting to current MAXED out setting this game still holds its place.
Gameplay - 10/10
Graphics - 9.5/10
Mechanics - 7.5/10
Story - 10/10
Lara Croft - 100/10

Shadow of the Tomb Raider is a disappointment after its superior predecessor, Rise of the Tomb Raider. Following on the heels of that game, this story does all the things that you'd expect from a Tomb Raider game: plenty of crypts and tombs with puzzles, running and gunning, and a little bit of story.

* STORY: Unfortunately, the story is uneven and predictable. The character dialog in particular is clunky and weak. The fact that the lead writer from RotTR left the company after that project is evident.
Props for trying to make some interesting storytelling with actual Mesoamerican culture and including surviving people who embrace that heritage. If only they were interesting to talk to.

* GAMEPLAY: Controls are extremely clunky and unpolished compared to the prior entry. Lara's movements are jerky and erratic, and the game frequently disregards inputs. Lara can run and leap straight on to a platform, only to be invisibly pushed sideways and to her death; some climbable surfaces suddenly can't be climbed when you need to, for no reason; jumping over obstacles sometimes results in Lara suddenly teleporting back into contact with the ground while at the height of a jump. All of these result in a cavalcade of frustrating deaths that don't make the tombs any more interesting to navigate, they just force you to keep starting over... and over... and over, because the controls are terrible.

* NEW SYSTEMS: The game introduces rappelling, so that Lara can rope down from above, and makes use of overhang climbs, where Lara climbs upside-down. Unfortunately, the camera work on the overhang climbs is inconsistent, so that sometimes it's impossible to tell where you're supposed to go while hanging upside-down like Batman. Rappelling would be interesting but its primary purpose is to swing places, but the controls for rappelling are also bad, like the others: You'll find yourself randomly switching between your movement controls climbing up and down the rope or swinging back and forth, resulting in Lara suddenly shortening her rope by climbing up when you're trying to swing and jump.
SotTR also makes use of many side missions, which you pick up in various hubs. Side missions, despite their innocuous nomenclature, can be quite lengthy. They do feel like they are not "side" missions but necessary upgrade missions, as they tend to lock pieces of gear or skill improvements.
Gunplay uses the usual pistols, rifles, shotguns, and bows, with various upgrades to unlock and outfits that provide various small boosts. Stealth gameplay now includes covering Lara in mud like Schwarzennegger in Predator and then hiding against muddy walls. Someone on the team really liked making Lara dirty, because she spends significant amounts of the game covered in mud.

* NEW GAME: This entry features a new game+ version, in which you keep your unlocks and then replay the game. Unlike RotTR, in which you finish the story and then can traverse the map and unlock all of the secrets, the gameplay in this is pointedly designed for you to NG+ it, because you can't access many areas due to missing pieces of gear at the time that you first find them, which means you have to progress further and then go back for them, or else speedrun the end, get all of the gear, and then NG+ for your "actual" playthrough. It's a frustrating attempt to make you play the game again to generate more hours of "content" without actually adding to the game.

* LAYOUT AND LEVEL DESIGN: The one area in which the game shines is, unsurprisingly, in its level design. Areas have lots of tight switchbacks and winding spaces, to help hide secrets and maximize the traversal space. Verticality is fairly common, and in a fitting concordance with Mesoamerican mythology, large chunks of the game take place in underwater cave systems. The vistas are beautiful (and staged with forced cameras, of course), and the levels provide interesting playspaces and a lot of room to search for hidden secrets. If only the core gameplay of traversing the levels were enjoyable!

Overall, this is a somewhat lackluster entry, with attempts to add new systems that feel rushed and unpolished, and a game that demands precision timed movement yet has extremely clunky and unresponsive controls. It almost feels like an indie platformer with a shined-up graphics engine.
The gameplay is good, the story is dumb. You have to turn off your brain to enjoy it.

I played the original Tomb Raider when it first cam out and it blew my mind. And this one has equally been mind blowing. It may be due to some misplaced nostalgic feeling that I have for the game but when i got washed away in the tsunami in the demo I was sold.

Story continues from the previous game "Rise of the Tomb Raider" but it's not crucial to have played it before, there are some small references here and there but would be better to have played it before this. Story is the best in this game compared to the others with stunning 2018 visuals. Some puzzles are in this game too but none of them are really complicated. Diving mechanics seems to have been a thing that the production studio was proud of because there is alot of swimming

The weapon upgrade and skill upgrade system is the best that it has been in all of the games. Same with crafting items. Overall a good game and I would buy it if it's on sale and if you are into singleplayer storygames.

Play game kill 5 enemies, Cutscene, Think I'm about to play game, Interactive cutscene where I hold W for 2 mins straight, Now maybe I play game?, Cutscene.


Many more things I can complain about but I'll leave you with a positive when you actually get to play the game its alright might be best in the new gen series so far.

While I originally hated this when it came out (mostly due to thinking the game was set in Egypt over Peru), I enjoyed my second play through of it more. I liked the puzzles and tombs in this game. I do think the initial ending pre-post credit cut scene still feels like a slap in the face to Tomb Raider fans depending on how you interpret Lara's last line. Overall the last 2 games of the prequel are the best.
i like the combat mechanics in the game and the enhanced graphics and puzzles... i recommend this game in 2026 and probably will still be good in the next 10 years
Its a decent port of the game. Sadly not optimized to run on Apple Silicon.  Will be a farewell with macOS 27

I really like the fact that you are really included in the culture in this game. The visuals are really immersive and the nature in this game feels so "calming" in a sense. It also has a very nice balance between action and story but it does get creepy at some parts.

so good so far, though i will say the movement is weirder (?) sort of like the older games- and i mean older!! as well as the surroundings and characters, during scenes its good detail but otherwise not so much (as expected as its not a game where you're worrying about looks, but i like having details! perhaps camera mode is better for this, yet to try.) though i will preface this by saying i am playing on laptop with my controller attached so probably thats whats making movement odd? :) all in all im loving the jungle vibes tbf, and lara looks like herself so thats a bonus :D kind of dreading the new game 2026 thats supposed to release as it doesnt look like lara from the past 2 games ive been on :( so this will have to do!

The game has frequent visual bugs, the story is poorly written, the characters are generic and uninteresting, the puzzles aren't anywhere as creative when compared to the first 2 games. There was a weird game breaking bug that prevented you from interacting with a puzzle unless you changed to 60 fps limit in the settings.

replaying Shadow of the Tomb Raider, felt pure nostalgia. The atmosphere, the Amazon jungle and Lara one of those characters you never forget. The game still hit exactly the same after all these years for me. out of the trilogy, this will always be my favorite.

Its a great game for exploration and the different environments make it enjoyable.  The only downside is the really difficult chanlenges which are either grossly difficult or really obscure the one with the raft was a pain

its tomb raider I guess, but to keep it short, as with any title that has a longer list of credited workers, the game is going to be proportionally worse. the original game had a team that could fit in one group photo. the team working on this title was almost 1.3k which naturally means a lot less actual talent and passion and more just assembly line workers manufacturing a product. puzzles are probably my least favorite aspect, second to the terrible dialogue ( i increased my difficulty just so she'd shut up about obvious clues, and usually turn off dialogue so I dont hear her useless extrapolations about the locations you visit and the incredibly dry story.)

Good game, more enjoyable than rise especially if you play on hard. There is a dumbass audio bug meaning half the time you can't hear Lara which sucks to try to work around. Other than that very fun and enjoyable experience especially if you can get it on sale.

is a masterfully crafted survival experience. It sacrifices some of the explosive, mainstream cinematic action of its predecessor to deliver a much truer "Tomb Raider" experience focused on isolating jungle survival, incredible stealth mechanics, and masterful puzzle design. It is a highly satisfying and gorgeous conclusion to Lara's origin story.

I thoroughly enjoyed the storyline of the game! The graphics are great too. I would love another game like this again or even like Rise of the Tomb Raider. Also bring back The Legend of Lara Croft back on Netflix, I really enjoyed it! Maybe with darker themes like in the games would be sick.

This is probably my favorite of the new games so far. I enjoyed the plot for what it was and was very grateful that it did not become a third run of ancient civilization fighting Lara who is fighting Trinity and blah blah.

This game is a mixed bag.

There are some things it does really well, and some things it does abysmally bad. Let's start with the good.

The actual level design, movement, and free-run sections are all really great. There's haptic feedback on controller with really helps to immerse you as the player and it is also done well and tastefully. The world and graphics themselves are gorgeous. The upgrade system and skill trees are also pretty good and help give a sense of progression. The story is okay, not the best but certainly acceptable. You get to find out about lara's backstory and there's even a section where you play as her as a kid which I personally found one of the most enjoyable moments in the game.

And now the bad.
My biggest gripe with this game is the combat. It is absolutely some of the most lifeless combat I have seen in any game. Your arrows and bullets seemingly have zero impact. Enemies do not react to being shot and once discovered will mindlessly drone towards you in straight lines. There's no physical recoil whatsoever, even when you shoot an enemy point blank with a shotgun. No reactions. Nothing. It's like shooting through paper. It's actually terrible. There are also only a few different types of enemies in the whole game and all of them patrol set paths with no deviations whatsoever. Absolutely lifeless. 

The game, like some of the other entries in the series, also suffer from being FAR too linear. Despite presenting itself as an "open world game" it could not be farther from it. It is linear to the point of feeling claustrophobic. The world doesn't offer much in terms of things to actually do besides maybe go into a little tomb here or explore a crypt there. It leaves a lot to be desired.

With all of that being said, I would actually recommend the game but only at a steep sale price. The movement, parkour, and free-running stuff is all great and really fun. If you are going into this game expecting good combat however, I would strongly recommend you do not purchase this game as you will be severely disappointed. 

Rise of the tomb raider is still the strongest edition of the series in my opinion.

8/10 basically same as the previous tomb rider game fun gameplay nice graphics story is lacking a bit 
solid game.

The second is the best, the third is good, and the first is best skipped.

Each game in the series has two problems:
- They're too long and have too many false endings—the scourge of the era.
- There's no acrobatics like in the very first games.
the third game of the rebooted franchise, always fun. Not too heavy for hardcore completitionist, making it a perfect game for singleplayer.
Played first 30 min. Game crashed. Reopened game "failed to load profile". Tried troubleshooting but all progress was lost. Uninstalled.

This game is over seven years old, but it's a lot of fun. Tons of puzzles. A leveling up system that improves gameplay and complexity yet feels intuitive. The weapons never feel awful to use. Story is pretty good. Overall spectacular for its time and still pretty good today.

8 years late to a game and I'm surprised it's still got as many bugs as it does. The game keeps changing resolutions as I'm playing and messing my screens up. And upon restarting the game to deal with the messed up resolutions, there's a chance all voice lines stop playing. I crawled under a door to get some loot and when I crawled out, the animation pushed me through the walls and the world. 

Technical issues aside, here are my gameplay gripes:
1) There are many jumps that require the player to be in the exact sweet spot before it deigns to perform the jump otherwise you just die.
2) It's difficult to tell where the next handhold is in some areas because there's lots of white decals surrounding the actual climb path.
3) Difficulty wayfinding in water, it's either too dark or completely murky.
4) Difficulty wayfinding in some tombs when it's both too bright and dusty.

The world looks nice, the set pieces look amazing. If this were a set of postcards, I would be happy. Unfortunately, it is a game and I must participate, and the amount of friction involved in just engaging with the systems is so high, I don't think I'm having fun any more.
Basically Uncharted but sexy. gameplay is smooth and story is cooked, Enjoyed the exploration and puzzle solving.

one of the best in the  Tomb Raider series, in my opinion. Also runs beautifully on Linux.

it's a more cinematic and actiony experience vs classic Tomb Raider, but there's still a lot of good puzzling fun to be had.

My one small complaint is they removed the Survival mode from previous entries, which was a nice 10 hours of fun post-campaign completion.

Shadow of the Tomb Raider usually gets flak for being an oddball in the Survivor trilogy due to its lack of combat and visual disparity. But this game really captures what Tomb Raider has always been about: tomb raiding. The series under Crystal Dynamics while good had slowly become a shameless copy of Uncharted with non-stop action and explosive set pieces, so SOTTR's return to form after so many years is a welcome change. Lara Croft now spends less time on murderous sprees and instead immerses herself in new cultures and explores ancient wonders, and you gotta love that feeling of whenever you enter a tomb, it genuinely feels like a foreboding descent into the underworld itself.

Sure, it's far from perfect. The story is full of holes, combat difficulty is still a big joke and gameplay can feel really clunky at times. But the thrill of exploration and solving intricate tombs while uncovering ancient civillizations makes this game a very unique experience. It may not be the most epic entry in the trilogy, but it's the one that captures the true spirit of Tomb Raider while still feeling new and grounded.

Half of the first 2 hours is just cutscenes, and half of the gameplay are just semi-cutscenes where you watch lara struggle while just holding down W. Somehow the menus and the skill tree upgrading are also just worse than in the previous game. I know the game will get better later when it actually lets you play, but I can't recommend a game that takes hours to be good.

EDIT: it did not get better. The last 3 hours were just me scrolling reddit while holding down W and doing a few parkour sections. Every encounter ends in Temple Run style escape sequence for some reason. Other than that it's just cutscenes, I don't get why this was made into a game when they clearly wanted to make this into a movie.

Does this game really feel that different from the other entries in the trilogy? Honestly, I couldn’t really tell. It took me almost 20 days to finally finish it.

At this point, games just don’t hit the same anymore after playing more than 200 of them — I guess that’s normal. Hopefully that feeling goes away eventually.

Is the game worth playing? Yeah, I’d say so. I personally didn’t feel too much repetition, but if I had tried to finish it in a short time, it probably would’ve become pretty boring.

And honestly… if it’s for Lara Croft, it’s worth it. Go play it.

As a long term Tomb Raider player, I would really say this game captures the thriller and adventure aspect of the game wonderfully. When I was younger and still played the very old pixelated versions on my mom's old desktop, there was always an aspect of the game that scared and amazed me simultaneously. "Rise of the Tomb Raider" didn't really speak to that for me, but this volume definitely does! I still miss Lara's old model, but other than that, love this game!

Great game with great visuals, and the tombs are actually way better designed than the previous 2 games. The combat is almost identical to the last one, just with some new guns that are more satisfying to use. If u played the past 2 entries u would enjoy this too. The plot isn't that much tbh, I was just wishing for a better ending. We will see about the Legacy of Atlantis soon, I just hope it will be smth revolutionary.

At the end, if u are someone who is seeking a balanced story with good combat but also not too long, its a great choice. 9/10.

Brilliant game, fitting conclusion for the trilogy. Beautiful environments, fun and challenging gameplay, still good for casual players with a short time for playing sessions. The story is once again excellent and I appreciate the "immersion mode" voice acting. The puzzles are probably the best in the trilogy.
My only issue and something I gotta warn new players about: I went into this game not knowing that the hidden city in Peru in this game's universe is not Inca but rather a blend of Inca and Maya cultures. I had to reframe my expectations since I was hoping for a more Inca setting when I saw this one takes place in Peru. It's not something that ruins the story or anything like that though of course.
Absolutely loving this game. I wish to have played this on an OLED monitor. But it is still so beautiful.
I loved this game! And I am excited to play another Lara Croft that's coming soon <3 Girl Power Punch!

For me personally this game did not motivate me to play beyond the main story despite having so much additional content (I also did not find the main story very well written). This was my first ever tomb raider game and I really enjoyed the climbing mechanics, but less the overall level design and other survival-esque game mechanics and the skill point system, as some of the abilities I would have never used anyway since they are poorly introduced in the game, so you just forget about them (at least from my view). The sound design was very haunting and I actually got scared sometimes so I will give it that. Probably a very good game for all the avid tomb raider fans, but not for me.

[i] A truly immersive gameplay, amazing locations, logical, puzzle-solving sidequests, and I could list more, but I wouldn't leave out our amazing protagonist, Lara Croft, who has already been through everything and survived, as a woman. [/i]

[h1] N O T E [/h1]
If you play, be very careful to start each sidequest by talking to the person who sent the sidequest first. An example of what I'm talking about: if you get to Paititi, i.e. The Hidden City, there will be a boy who will give you a sidequest, his name is Takiy. So
The glitch is activated if, after loading the game, you talk to Pisco first, before talking to the little boy who started the quest, Takiy. Pisco is then in an invisible, but still interactive state in his house. If you interact with him during this phase, the logic of the mission will collapse fatally: he will send you to the outcasts, but after you have spoken to all five, Pisco will disappear or become invisible for good, and the game will not offer the interaction to complete the mission.

I have played a lot of Tomb Raider games, and each one of them are amazing, including this one.
PS: Lara Croft is Gorgeous as ♥♥♥♥ man.
